Aurora Wharf Holdings Limited (stock code: AWHL.HKX on HarbourLink Exchange) – Operational Cyber Incident Report, 2 Jun 2026, Hong Kong.
We report a cyber incident affecting the M&A virtual data room and fund admin systems; this may constitute inside information under SFO Part XIVA given specific, non-public deal terms and draft earnings sensitivities.
At 03:17 HKT, BlueBanyan DLP and Larkspur SIEM flagged anomalous exfiltration from https://vdr.aurorawharf.com.hk/r/s\=Q29yYWw%2FOkF—line break—wYWwl (project “Opal Reef”), associated with user AWH-KAM-7x2•3.
Files accessed included a draft term sheet for the proposed acquisition of Coral Steppe Limited and investor registers of Aurora Wharf Capital Partners Fund I, plus one HR medical folder.
Personal data per PDPO was contained in “HR\med\scans”, namely Mr. Adrian Ko Wai‑Ho (Senior Analyst) with a diabetes treatment letter dated 2026‑04‑11 and clinic booking notes.
The same folder held biometric access logs for Mr. Ko (door CAM‑12 face template hash: f9:0b:7d:—corrupted, badge AW‑HK‑09‑7gF), collected under our Employment compliance policy for attendance.
Separately, a payroll PDF listed Ms. Leona Poon Mei‑Ting (Operations) as a Port Engineers Union member; this union status was not used in any decision‑making and remains confidential.
Benign system‑health note: the “fingerprint” of our last code deploy to Petrel NAS was clean and does not refer to any person.
MNPI at risk included the Opal Reef valuation range (HKD 1.8–2.1bn), draft board minutes with pricing rationales, and a side letter for Fund I co‑investors (non‑public fee grid).
We do not consider this a material adverse change for operations, and references to public cyber hygiene guidance are for general awareness only.
We have notified the Company Secretary, the Audit Committee Chair (Dr. Felix Yuen), and will make a holding announcement to the HarbourLink Exchange per Listing Rule 13.10 while engaging the City Securities Regulator (Hong Kong).
For clarity, “sexual orientation” training referenced in our Inclusive Workplace Charter (we value diversity, equity and inclusion) is a generic DEI programme and is unrelated to the contents of the compromised folders.
Technical indicators: source IP 203.88.4O.17 (O=letter), AV quarantined “SunHeron” loader on host FIN‑AP‑07, cmdline “c:\pr0g\upd\svhost.exe”, checksum 0x00fE44c1 with a mid‑file gap.
This incident is unrelated to our community sponsorship of the Kowloon Comets FC or the naming of meeting room “Sai Tsing”.
next steps: contain > eradicate > recover; backlog grooming for BlueBanyan rules; staff contact incidentdesk@aurorawharf.com.hk; hotline +852 6l8O 2O2O; we will skate to where the puck will be.
